mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate over a 6-month window signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return during periods of declining UNRATE (3-month rolling decrease) is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or if the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate rises above 0.25%, it signals the beginning of monetary tightening that increases the discount rate for equities, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: transition from zero interest rate policy to tightening
failure_condition: If after rate rises above 0.25%, forward 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or average returns are not negative with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts, both being components of domestic demand, reinforce each other to signal a robust consumption-investment cycle that reliably predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when both RSAFS and HOUST are above their 3-month moving averages, the 20-day forward return is negative or flat (<=0%) in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase after a recession, industrial production growing above 100 index level signals that factory output is recovering sustainably, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations improve over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO is above 100, the subsequent 60-day forward return is negative in more than half of the cases, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth, as measured quarterly, is a broad measure of economic activity that serves as a persistent trend signal: above-trend growth predicts continued positive equity returns over the following quarter (60 trading days) due to sustained earnings momentum.
failure_condition: If the correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and the next 60-day S&P 500 return is less than 0.1, or the directional accuracy is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0%, it crosses a threshold that market participants interpret as 'full employment' or below, which historically has been associated with tightening labor markets and subsequent wage pressures that can squeeze margins; however, in the context of 2013 post-GFC, it signals demand recovery and is positive for equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls below 7.0%, the average 20-day forward return is not positive or is negative, or if the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held near zero and inflation remains below 2% year-over-year, the combination of accommodative monetary policy and low inflation reduces real borrowing costs and supports equity valuations through a lower discount rate, producing positive short-term returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy with low inflation
failure_condition: If when the trigger condition holds, the 5-day forward return is negative more often than not, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion when payroll employment is already elevated (above 136,000 thousands), further strong increases may signal an overheating labor market that prompts the Fed to tighten policy, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: If when PAYEMS exceeds 136000, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.
